description: Academic papers from top-tier venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, ACL, IEEE S&P, Science, arXiv) that provide independent, peer-reviewed evidence for AI tool recommendations.

papers.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
papers.key_finding: The best model solved only 4.8% of real GitHub issues at launch, revealing a large gap between toy benchmarks and real-world software engineering.
papers.implication: Justifies why agentic tools (Claude Code, Cursor) are needed on top of raw LLMs.

papers.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
papers.key_finding: Agent interface design matters as much as model capability. A custom ACI achieves 12.5% on SWE-bench vs 4.8% with raw API.
papers.implication: Tools with good agent scaffolding (Claude Code, Cursor Agent) outperform raw API access.

papers.title: Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code (Codex / HumanEval)
papers.key_finding: Repeated sampling (pass@100) dramatically outperforms single-shot generation. Foundational evaluation methodology for code LLMs.
papers.implication: Tools offering multiple suggestions (Copilot, Codeium) are backed by this principle.

papers.title: Is Your Code Generated by ChatGPT Really Correct? Rigorous Evaluation of LLMs for Code Generation
papers.key_finding: With rigorous tests, GPT-4 pass rate drops from 67% to 51.2%. LLM-generated code often passes weak tests but contains subtle bugs.
papers.implication: Justifies recommending robust testing alongside AI code generation.

papers.title: RepairAgent: An Autonomous, LLM-Based Agent for Program Repair
papers.key_finding: LLM agent with tool use fixes 164/835 bugs, significantly outperforming non-LLM repair tools.
papers.implication: LLMs + tool use outperform standalone approaches for bug fixing.

papers.title: The Effectiveness of AI Code Assistants in Real-World Software Development
papers.key_finding: Developers accept ~25-30% of AI suggestions. Highest acceptance for boilerplate and test code. Code quality remains stable.
papers.implication: AI code assistants are most useful for repetitive code and tests.

papers.title: CodeGen: An Open Large Language Model for Code with Multi-Turn Program Synthesis
papers.key_finding: Multi-turn conversational code generation outperforms single-turn generation for complex tasks.
papers.implication: Supports chat-based coding tools (Claude, ChatGPT) over one-shot autocomplete for complex tasks.

papers.title: Competition-Level Code Generation with AlphaCode
papers.key_finding: Achieves median human-level in competitive programming via massive sampling + filtering.
papers.implication: Generate-and-filter paradigm validates tools using multiple completions.

papers.title: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback (InstructGPT/RLHF)
papers.key_finding: RLHF makes a 1.3B model preferred over 175B GPT-3. Alignment through human feedback is critical for useful AI assistants.
papers.implication: Explains WHY instruction-tuned models (GPT-4, Claude) are better for dev tools than base models.

papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
papers.key_finding: AI can self-supervise alignment using constitutional principles, improving both harmlessness and helpfulness.
papers.implication: Explains Claude's safety approach. Relevant for security-sensitive code generation.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.key_finding: Intermediate reasoning steps dramatically improve performance on multi-step tasks, with gains scaling with model size.
papers.implication: Explains why 'thinking' models produce better code for complex algorithmic tasks.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.key_finding: LLM performance follows predictable power-law scaling with compute, data, and parameters.
papers.implication: Explains why newer/larger models consistently outperform on coding tasks.

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.authors: Lewis, Perez, Piktus et al. (Meta AI / UCL)
papers.key_finding: Combining retrieval with generation produces more factual outputs. Establishes the RAG paradigm.
papers.implication: Explains why RAG-enhanced coding tools (Cursor, Cody) outperform context-limited ones.

papers.title: Let's Verify Step by Step
papers.key_finding: Process-based reward models (verifying each step) outperform outcome-based models: 78.2% vs 72.4% on MATH.
papers.implication: Explains reasoning improvements in thinking models. Justifies recommending them for debugging.

papers.title: Chatbot Arena: An Open Platform for Evaluating LLMs by Human Preference
papers.authors: Chiang, Zheng, Sheng et al. (LMSYS / UC Berkeley)
papers.key_finding: Crowdsourced pairwise evaluation with Elo ratings is more reliable than static benchmarks. 1M+ human votes reveal differences invisible in automated metrics.
papers.implication: Justifies using Arena rankings as primary signal for tool recommendations.

papers.title: Measuring Massive Multitask Language Understanding (MMLU)
papers.key_finding: 57-subject benchmark establishing the primary metric for comparing general LLM capabilities.
papers.implication: Foundation benchmark for comparing models on general-purpose developer assistance.

papers.title: LiveCodeBench: Holistic and Contamination-Free Evaluation of LLMs for Code
papers.key_finding: Models' HumanEval scores are inflated by 10-20% due to benchmark contamination. Continuously updated problems prevent this.
papers.implication: Cautions against relying on HumanEval alone. Supports using multiple benchmarks.

papers.title: CRUXEval: A Benchmark for Code Reasoning, Understanding, and Execution
papers.key_finding: GPT-4 achieves only ~70% on execution prediction. Even top models struggle with precise code semantics.
papers.implication: Supports recommending human review of AI-generated code.

papers.title: Design2Code: How Far Are We From Automating Front-End Engineering?
papers.key_finding: GPT-4V generates HTML/CSS from screenshots with 49% visual similarity. Multimodal models outperform text-only by 2x on UI-to-code.
papers.implication: Justifies recommending multimodal AI tools for frontend prototyping.

papers.title: WebSight: A Comprehensive Dataset for Automated Web Page Generation
papers.key_finding: 2M webpage screenshot-code pairs enable small models to generate HTML competitively with GPT-4V.
papers.implication: Supports feasibility of specialized tools for UI generation like v0.

safety_reliability.name: AI Safety & Reliability for Production Code

papers.title: Asleep at the Keyboard? Assessing the Security of GitHub Copilot's Code Contributions
papers.key_finding: ~40% of Copilot-generated code for security-relevant scenarios contains vulnerabilities.
papers.implication: CRITICAL: Security review is mandatory alongside AI code generation.

papers.title: Do Users Write More Insecure Code with AI Assistants?
papers.key_finding: Developers using AI assistants produced MORE security vulnerabilities AND were MORE confident their code was correct.
papers.implication: AI tools can create false confidence. Security scanning is non-negotiable.

papers.title: A Survey on Hallucination in Large Language Models
papers.key_finding: Larger models hallucinate less frequently but not less confidently. RAG and self-consistency checks mitigate this.
papers.implication: Justifies recommending tools with RAG/grounding over raw generation.

papers.title: The Impact of AI on Developer Productivity: Evidence from GitHub Copilot
papers.authors: Peng, Kalliamvakou, Cihon, Demirer (GitHub / Microsoft Research)
papers.key_finding: Randomized controlled trial: Copilot users completed tasks 55.8% faster. Effect concentrated on less experienced developers.
papers.implication: Strongest empirical evidence for AI coding tool productivity gains.

papers.title: Large Language Models for Software Engineering: A Systematic Literature Review
papers.key_finding: Review of 200+ papers: LLMs most effective for code generation (>70% improvement), moderately for testing/docs, least mature for requirements/design.
papers.implication: Evidence-based hierarchy: AI is best for coding, good for testing, growing for docs, emerging for planning.

papers.title: Expectation vs. Experience: Evaluating the Usability of Code Generation Tools Powered by LLMs
papers.key_finding: Time spent understanding and debugging AI code offsets productivity gains for complex tasks. Simple tasks see net benefit.
papers.implication: AI tools are best for well-defined subtasks, not entire complex implementations.

papers.title: LLM-Based Test Generation: An Industrial Case Study at Google
papers.key_finding: LLM-generated tests achieve comparable coverage to human-written tests for ~70% of functions.
papers.implication: Supports recommending AI for test generation with human oversight for complex tests.

key_insights_for_recommendations.code_generation: Agentic tools with repo context outperform raw APIs by 2-3x on real tasks (SWE-bench, ICLR 2024). 55.8% faster development in RCT (Microsoft Research).
key_insights_for_recommendations.testing_qa: AI tests match human coverage for 70% of functions (Google, ICSE 2024). But 40% of AI code has security vulns (IEEE S&P 2022) — testing is mandatory.
key_insights_for_recommendations.ui_generation: Multimodal models achieve 49% visual fidelity on screenshot-to-code (Design2Code). Good for prototyping, needs human refinement.
key_insights_for_recommendations.requirements_analysis: LLMs least mature here per 200+ paper review (ACM TOSEM 2024). Chain-of-thought models best for complex reasoning (NeurIPS 2022).
key_insights_for_recommendations.documentation: RAG-enhanced tools produce more factual output (NeurIPS 2020). Larger models hallucinate less frequently (ACL 2023).
key_insights_for_recommendations.security_warning: Developers using AI produce MORE vulnerabilities AND are MORE confident code is secure (ACM CCS 2023, Stanford). Security scanning is non-negotiable.
